schemas.name: Virtual Auditor

step.text: File GSTR-1 by 11th of next month

mainEntity.name: What is the due date for GSTR-3B?

acceptedAnswer.text: Monthly filers: 20th of the following month. QRMP scheme filers: 22nd/24th of the month following the quarter. Late fee: ₹50/day (₹20 for nil return), capped at ₹5,000 per return period.

GSTR-2A is a dynamic document showing all invoices uploaded by your suppliers. GSTR-2B is the static monthly statement. Reconciliation ensures your ITC claims match supplier-uploaded data — mismatches can lead to ITC denial.

mainEntity.name: What GST returns need to be filed monthly?

acceptedAnswer.text: GSTR-1: outward supply details, due by 11th of next month. GSTR-3B: summary return with tax payment, due by 20th. IFF (Invoice Furnishing Facility): optional for QRMP scheme taxpayers. GSTR-2B: auto-generated, no filing required.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late GST return filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Late fee: ₹50/day (₹25 CGST + ₹25 SGST) per return, capped at ₹5,000. Nil returns: ₹20/day. Interest on late tax payment: 18% p.a. under Section 50. Input tax credit blocked if GSTR-3B not filed for 2+ consecutive periods.

mainEntity.name: What is QRMP scheme in GST?

acceptedAnswer.text: Quarterly Return Monthly Payment scheme for taxpayers with turnover up to ₹5 crore. File GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B quarterly instead of monthly. Pay tax monthly through challan (PMT-06). Effective from January 2021.

mainEntity.name: How to file nil GST return?

acceptedAnswer.text: If no sales, no purchases, and no tax liability in a period, file nil GSTR-1 and nil GSTR-3B. Can be filed via SMS from registered mobile number. Still mandatory even if business is inactive. Late fee ₹20/day for late nil returns.

Yearly consolidated return due by December 31. Mandatory for all regular taxpayers. GSTR-9C (reconciliation statement) required if turnover exceeds ₹5 crore. Must reconcile with audited financial statements.

faqs.name: What is the due date for GSTR-3B?

acceptedAnswer.text: Monthly filers: 20th of the following month. QRMP scheme filers: 22nd/24th of the month following the quarter. Late fee: ₹50/day (₹20 for nil return), capped at ₹5,000 per return period.

GSTR-2A is a dynamic document showing all invoices uploaded by your suppliers. GSTR-2B is the static monthly statement. Reconciliation ensures your ITC claims match supplier-uploaded data — mismatches can lead to ITC denial.

acceptedAnswer.text: Quarterly Return Monthly Payment scheme for taxpayers with turnover up to ₹5 crore. File GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B quarterly instead of monthly. Pay tax monthly through challan (PMT-06). Effective from January 2021.
